justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical with detailed reproduction scenario; jsonwebtoken CVE-2022-23529 flagged as Critical with CVSS score; 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string ESM, left-pad supply chain, moment deprecation); safe_to_deploy is false; severity ratings are well-differentiated (Critical for stripe and JWT, High for left-pad, Important for moment/crypto-random-string/pg, Suggestion-level for engines/lockfile).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard identified as Critical with excellent detail on specific breaking changes across major versions
jsonwebtoken CVE-2022-23529 cited by number with CVSS 7.6 score and PCI-DSS compliance implications
crypto-random-string ESM-only issue correctly identified with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM error and CJS fallback recommendation
left-pad supply chain risk flagged with historical context and native padStart replacement
moment deprecation noted with size and ReDoS concerns, recommending date-fns or dayjs
Went beyond requirements to flag pg version looseness, missing engines field, and missing lockfile
Actionable fixes provided for every finding with estimated time to fix
Escalation to Bird for PCI-DSS compliance is appropriate cross-team routing

### Summary

**Verdict: BLOCK**

This payment-processing service has dependency configurations that create real production failure modes — including a wildcard version on the core payment SDK, a known-vulnerable auth library, and an unnecessary transitive-risk dependency.
